message:
🌱 Add alertmanager: Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with mission (#1720)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch: @@ -0,0 +1,76 @@ +{ + "version": "kc-mission-v1", + "name": "alertmanager-2923-telegram-can-t-parse-entities-after-message-truncation-with-pa", + "missionClass": "solution", + "author": "KubeStellar Bot", + "authorGithub": "kubestellar", + "mission": { + "title": "alertmanager: Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with parse_mode="HTML"", + "description": "Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with parse_mode="HTML". This issue affects 23+ users.", + "type": "troubleshoot", + "status": "completed", + "steps": [ + { + "title": "Identify alertmanager troubleshoot symptoms", + "description": "Check for the issue in your alertmanager deployment: ```bash kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=100 | grep -i error ``` Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue." + }, + { + "title": "Review alertmanager configuration", + "description": "Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration: ```bash kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager ``` **What did you do?** Used Telegram integration with parse_mode="HTML" **What did you expect to see?** Alert notification in Telegram chat **What did you see instead? Under which circumstances?** Alert notification wasn't sent to chat * System" + }, + { + "title": "Apply the fix for Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with…", + "description": "I used [this workaround](https://github.com/pando85/homelab/blob/a8fd225df671dffa9b1b619d495ff249afecc99c/system/monitoring/resources/alertmanager-telegram-template.yaml#L30-L43) until we have a better solution: ```gotemplate {{- $alertsLen := (len .Alerts) -}} {{ range .Alerts ```yaml global: resolve_timeout: 3m templates: - '/etc/alertmanager/templates/*.tmpl' receivers: - name: alerts-test1 telegram_configs: - api_url: https://api.telegram.org bot_token: 1657974151:BBBO9Iggg_Tif04Tg9asdfSuR83niyNOSHE # has been changed for security reasons chat_id: -10075044612354 # has been changed for security reasons disable_notifications: false message: '{{ template "telegram.html.message" . }}' parse_mode: HTML send_resolved: true route: group_by: - alertname group_interval: 30s group_wait: 15s receiver: alerts-test1 repeat_interval: 1d ```" + }, + { + "title": "Confirm Telegram can't parse entities after message… is resolved", + "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs: ```bash kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=50 --since=5m kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10 ``` Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone." + } + ], + "resolution": { + "summary": "I used [this workaround](https://github.com/pando85/homelab/blob/a8fd225df671dffa9b1b619d495ff249afecc99c/system/monitoring/resources/alertmanager-telegram-template.yaml#L30-L43) until we have a better solution: ```gotemplate {{- $alertsLen := (len .Alerts) -}} {{ range .Alerts }} {{- if .Annotations.description }} _{{ .Annotations.description }}_ {{-", + "codeSnippets": [ + "global: resolve_timeout: 3m templates: - '/etc/alertmanager/templates/*.tmpl' receivers: - name: alerts-test1 telegram_configs: - api_url: https://api.telegram.org bot_token: # has been changed for security reasons chat_id: -10075044612354 # has been changed for security reasons disable_notifications: false message: '{{ template "telegram.html.message" . }}' parse_mode: HTML send_resolved: true route: group_by: - alertname group_interval: 30s group_wait: 15s receiver: alerts-test1 repeat_interval: 1d", + "{{ define "telegram.html.message" }} {{ range .Alerts }} {{ if eq .Status "firing"}}🔥 {{ .Labels.alertname }} 🔥{{ else }}✅ {{ .Labels.alertname }} ✅{{ end }} Labels:{{ range $key, $value := .Labels }}{{ if ne $key "alertname" }} {{ $key }}: {{ $value }}{{ end }}{{ end }} Annotations:{{ range $key, $value := .Annotations }} {{ $key }}: {{ $value }}{{ end }} {{ end }} {{ end }}", + "May 16 11:21:59 focal-1 alertmanager[30906]: ts=2022-05-16T08:21:59.430Z caller=telegram.go:72 level=debug integration=telegram msg="truncated message" truncated_message="\n\n🔥 ExporterDown 🔥\n Labels:\n address: 192.168.56.10\n environment: testing\n instance: 192.168.56.10:9100\n job: node_exporter\n label10 : valuevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevalue\n label11: valuevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevalue\n label12: valuevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevalu evaluevalue\n label13: valuevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevalue\n label2: valuevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevaluevalue\n label3" + ] + } + }, + "metadata": { + "tags": [ + "alertmanager", + "graduated", + "observability", + "troubleshoot" + ], + "cncfProjects": [ + "alertmanager" + ], + "targetResourceKinds": [], + "difficulty": "beginner", + "issueTypes": [ + "troubleshoot" + ], + "maturity": "graduated", + "sourceUrls": { + "issue": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/issues/2923", + "repo": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager" + }, + "reactions": 23, + "comments": 11, + "synthesizedBy": "copilot" + }, + "prerequisites": { + "kubernetes": ">=1.24", + "tools": [ + "kubectl" + ], + "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible." + }, + "security": { + "scannedAt": "2026-03-24T06:30:02.648Z", + "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0", + "sanitized": true, + "findings": [] + } +}
